title: Irish stew
instructions: Heat the oven to 180C/350F/gas 4.
Drain and rinse the soaked wheat, cover with plenty of water, bring to a boil and simmer about 1 hour until tender. Drain and set aside.

Season the lamb with 1 tsp salt and black pepper. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large deep saute pan over medium-high heat, and sear the lamb in batches for 4 minutes until browned all over, adding oil as needed. Transfer to a bowl.
Lower to medium heat, add 1 tbsp oil, and fry the shallots 4 minutes until caramelised; add to the lamb. Brown the remaining vegetables the same way, adding oil as needed.
Add the wine, sugar, herbs, 1 tsp salt and a good grind of pepper to the pan and boil hard for 3 minutes.
Return the lamb, vegetables and wheat to the pot, add the stock, cover and boil for 5 minutes.
Transfer to the oven and cook, covered, for 1 hour 30 minutes.
Check the liquid; if there is a lot, uncover and boil a few minutes to reduce.

ingredients.original: 300g soaked overnight in water whole wheat
2kg cut into 3cm cubes lamb loin chops

ingredients.note: 120ml olive oil converted at ~0.9 g/ml; used in batches to sear lamb and brown vegetables and mostly left in the pan
count only, no weight given; 24 shallots estimated at ~30g each
count only, no weight given; 4 large carrots estimated at ~120g each
count only, no weight given; 2 turnips estimated at ~140g each
count only, no weight given; 1 celeriac estimated at ~650g

title: Choc Chip Pecan Pie
instructions: Make the pastry: tip the flour, butter, cream cheese and icing sugar into a food processor with 1/4 tsp salt and blend until it looks like breadcrumbs. With the blade running, drizzle in 2-3 tsp cold water until the mixture clumps together. Bring together into a ball, pat into a disc, wrap and chill at least 20 mins.
Heat oven to 200C/180C fan. Roll the pastry to the thickness of a coin and line a deep 23cm fluted tart tin, pressing into the corners with no gaps. Leave 1cm overhanging and keep the scraps.

Line the case with scrunched parchment and baking beans. Blind-bake 15-20 mins until the sides are set, then remove the beans and parchment and bake 5 mins more until golden. Trim the pastry flush with the tin and patch any cracks with the scraps.
In a pan, melt the butter, maple syrup, both sugars and 1/4 tsp salt, stirring until smooth. Take off the heat and cool 10 mins. Reduce oven to 160C/140C fan.
Beat the eggs, then mix in the syrup mixture, vanilla and pecans until well combined.
Pour half the filling into the case, scatter over half the chocolate chips, then add the rest of the filling and chips. Bake on the middle shelf 50-55 mins until set.
Cool, then chill at least 2 hrs before serving.

title: Battenberg Cake
instructions: Heat oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. Line the base and sides of a 20cm square tin with baking parchment, crossing two 20cm strips over the base.
Make the almond sponge: beat butter, caster sugar, self-raising flour, ground almonds, baking powder, eggs, vanilla and almond extract in a large bowl with an electric whisk until smooth.
Scrape into the tin, spread to the corners and bake 25-30 mins, until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tin 10 mins, then finish on a wire rack.
Make the pink sponge the same way but omit the almond extract and fold in pink food colouring. Bake and cool as before.
Heat the apricot jam until runny, then sieve. Trim the sponges square, then cut each into 4 slices the same width as the sponge height. Trim to equal lengths.
Roll one marzipan block on icing-sugar-dusted surface to just over 20cm wide and about 0.5cm thick. Brush with jam.

Lay a pink and an almond slice side by side on the marzipan, jam between them, leaving 4cm marzipan clear at the end. Brush jam on top and sandwich the remaining 2 slices on, alternating colours for a checkerboard.
Trim marzipan to length, lift and smooth over the cake, leaving a small fold along the bottom edge. Crimp the edges with fingers or a fork and mark the 10 slices.
Repeat to assemble the second Battenberg. Keep airtight or wrapped up to 3 days, or freeze up to a month.

ingredients.note: Instructions specify ground almonds; mapped to ground almonds key.
Instructions specify ground almonds; mapped to ground almonds key.
No usdaTable key for food colouring; ½ tsp inferred as ~2.5g.
Instructions use apricot jam ('heat the jam'); mapped to apricot jam key.
No amount stated; dusting inferred as ~10g.

title: Jerk chicken with rice & peas
instructions: Blitz the ginger, garlic, onion, spring onions, chilli, thyme, lime juice, soy sauce, oil, brown sugar, allspice and a teaspoon of salt in a food processor until it forms a thick, smooth jerk paste — pause to scrape down the sides if it stalls, but don't add water.
Taste the paste and balance it: it should read pleasantly salty rather than sharp, so add more chilli for heat or a touch more brown sugar if it's too salty or sour.
Slash the chicken thighs a few times, then rub the jerk paste all over and into the cuts.
Cover and marinate the chicken in the fridge overnight.
For a barbecue: light the coals an hour ahead, add wood chips for smoke, and cook the chicken over slow, indirect heat for 30 minutes.
For the oven: heat to 180C/160C fan/gas 4, put the chicken in a roasting tin with the lime halves, and roast for 45 minutes until cooked through.
While the chicken cooks, rinse the rice thoroughly, then tip it into a saucepan with the coconut milk, spring onions, thyme and garlic, keeping the kidney beans aside for later.
Season with salt, add 300ml cold water, bring to the boil, then reduce to medium, cover and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the kidney beans, cover and leave off the heat for 5 minutes until the liquid is absorbed.
Squeeze the roasted lime over the chicken and serve with the rice and peas, plus hot sauce on the side if you like it fiery.

ingredients.note: no per-piece weight given; assumed ~120g per thigh x12
half a lime roasted alongside chicken then squeezed over; weight inferred from average lime
half a medium onion, weight inferred
juice of one lime, weight inferred
oil is part of the marinade and stays on the meat as served, not a frying medium
assumed pre-cooked/canned beans stirred in near the end and only warmed through

title: Lamingtons
instructions: Heat the oven to 200C/180C fan/gas 6. Butter and line a 20 x 30cm rectangular tin.

Beat the salted butter and golden caster sugar until pale and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each. Beat in the flour, milk and salt until combined, then spoon into the tin. Bake for 25 minutes until golden and firm. Cool completely.

Slice the sponge horizontally into two halves and trim the edges square. Cut into 18 squares. Whip the cream with the icing sugar to soft peaks. Spread jam then cream on half the squares, sandwich with the remaining squares, and chill in the fridge.

For the icing, whisk the melted unsalted butter with the milk. Sieve the cocoa powder and icing sugar together, then gradually whisk into the butter mixture until smooth. Blend with a hand blender if it turns lumpy.
Divide the desiccated coconut between three shallow bowls.
Dip each lamington in the icing until fully coated, roll in the coconut and set on a wire rack. Chill at least 1 hour.

title: Spanish-style slow-cooked lamb shoulder & beans
instructions: Mix all the spice ingredients together with a generous pinch of salt to make a rub.
Score the lamb shoulder deeply all over and massage the spice rub into the cuts.
Marinate for up to 24 hours if you have time, though it's fine to cook straight away.
Preheat the oven to 150°C fan 130°C gas 2.
Heat the olive oil in a large flameproof casserole over medium-high heat.
Add the onions, carrots and garlic bulb and sizzle for 5 minutes until softened.
Pour in the chicken stock and bring to the boil.
Nestle the lamb into the pan, cover, and roast in the oven for 2 hours.
Uncover and lift the lamb onto a plate.
Stir the butter beans, roasted peppers and olives through the braising liquid.
Sit the lamb back on top, return uncovered to the oven, and roast for a further 1 hour 30 minutes until cooked through.
Transfer the lamb to a board and shred with two forks.
Stir the parsley through the beans before serving.

ingredients.note: No yield stated in recipe; servings inferred as 8 based on total food mass (~5.6kg) at roughly 700g per serving.
1 tbsp olive oil ≈14g, inferred conversion; oil stays in the braise, not a frying medium.
4 tbsp chopped parsley ≈4g each, inferred weight.
4 cloves ≈3g each, inferred weight; part of spice rub, stays on lamb.
4 tbsp olive oil ≈14g each, inferred weight; part of spice rub, remains on lamb, not a frying medium.

title: Baklava with spiced nuts, ricotta & chocolate
instructions: Make the syrup: combine the sugar with 650ml water in a large saucepan. Stir over low heat until dissolved, then boil. Reduce to a simmer for 15 mins, add a few drops of lemon juice and simmer 5 mins more. Cool.
Melt the butter gently in a small pan for 5 mins, skimming off any froth. Set aside for assembly.
For the filling, crush all the nuts to a mostly-fine mix with a few chunky pieces. Stir in the cinnamon and cardamom and set aside.
In another bowl, mix the ricotta with the lemon and orange zests and the vanilla.
Heat the oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4. Brush a large baking tray (about 35 x 47cm) with melted butter.
Working one filo sheet at a time (keep the rest under a damp tea towel), lay a sheet with a short end facing you. Scatter 30g of the nut mix over it, then spoon 1 tbsp ricotta along the near end.

Fold the near end over the filling, set a thin skewer alongside and roll into a long, chipolata-thick roll. Scrunch it accordion-style, slide it off the skewer onto the tray, and repeat for about 12 rolls.
Cut each roll into four for 48 large baklava (or eight for 96 mini). Brush with the remaining butter and bake 20-25 mins until golden, turning the tray halfway.
While the baklava is still hot, ladle over the syrup until it sizzles. Leave to cool and absorb.
Melt the dark chocolate over simmering water or in short microwave bursts, drizzle over the cooled baklava and finish with the ground pistachios.

ingredients.note: Recipe says 'a few drops' with no amount; inferred ~3g.
Inferred from 2 tbsp ground cinnamon at ~7g/tbsp.
Inferred from 1 tbsp ground cardamom at ~6g/tbsp.

title: Classic Tourtière
instructions: Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high and saute the onion and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the ground beef and pork with the summer savoury, clove and nutmeg. Cook, breaking up the meat, until browned.
Pour in the beef stock and bring to a simmer, then lower to medium-low. Stir in the grated potato and cook until the liquid is almost fully absorbed, about 15 minutes.
Remove the bay leaf, season with salt and pepper, then take off the heat and let the filling cool completely — it bakes best chilled.
Heat the oven to 375F (190C). Roll one pastry disc just under 1/4 inch thick and line a 9-inch pie plate. Fill with cooled tourtiere mixture and spread even.
Roll a second disc to the same thickness, cut a steam hole in the centre, and lay it over the filling. Trim to 1/2 inch beyond the rim and pinch the edges to seal. Brush with beaten egg.
Bake about 45 minutes, until the pastry is deep golden. Let rest 15 minutes before slicing. Repeat with the remaining pastry for the second pie.

ingredients.note: no size given; assumed one medium onion

ingredients.original: 1 tsp Dried Leaves Of Summer Savoury
ingredients.note: weight inferred from 1 tsp dried herb

title: Flija Layered Pancake / Crepe
instructions: Whisk the flour, water, and salt together until every lump dissolves; the batter should be thick but still pourable — add a splash more water if it's too stiff.
Set the flour batter aside.
Whisk the melted butter, oil, and your chosen yogurt or cream together until smooth; let it rest 10 minutes, then whisk again until it's the consistency of custard.
Set the filling aside.
Preheat the grill to a medium setting.
Rub a little olive oil over the base of a large, deep baking tray or pie pan.
Pipe or ladle the flour batter in thin lines across the pan in a star pattern, leaving triangular gaps between the lines — the batter is too thick to pour.
Grill this first layer until golden brown, 3–5 minutes.
Brush the whole surface, both battered and bare patches, with a layer of the filling mixture.
Pipe another star-patterned layer of flour batter, threading it through the gaps left by the previous layer and again leaving triangular spaces.
Grill until golden, another 3–5 minutes.
Repeat the filling-then-batter layering, topping up any thin or bare patches every couple of rounds, until all the batter and filling are used up — this takes roughly 2 hours total.
Serve the finished flija straight away with honey or yogurt.

ingredients.note: chosen filling variant (yogurt-based); brushed into the layers and eaten, stays in dish
200ml converted to grams using oil density ~0.92g/ml; part of chosen filling variant
5 tbsp (~15ml each) converted to grams; recipe says 'chosen yogurt/cream' — three filling variants given, this one (yogurt) chosen as the canonical filling, others zeroed
alternative filling variant (clotted cream); not summed — cook picks one filling, not all three
alternative filling variant (clotted cream); not summed
alternative filling variant; not summed — 'chosen yogurt/cream' implies one filling is used

title: Mini butter buns
instructions: Whisk together the bread flour, sugar, yeast, and salt in a large bowl.
Pour in the warm milk and knead until a rough dough forms.
Once the dough turns smooth and elastic, work in the softened butter and keep kneading until fully incorporated.
Cover the bowl and let the dough rise until it has doubled in size, about 90 minutes.
Turn the risen dough out onto a floured surface, divide it into small portions, and roll each into a smooth ball.
Grease your baking pans lightly and arrange the dough balls on them.
Cover the pans and let the buns rise again until they look puffy and soft.
Brush the tops of the risen buns with egg white using a pastry brush.
Near the end of the rise, preheat the oven to 375°F.
Uncover the buns and bake for 28 to 32 minutes, until the crusts turn golden brown.
Pull them from the oven and enjoy warm.

ingredients.note: Original states a range '4-5 pound Bread Flour'; took low end (4 lb ≈ 1814g). No yield stated for this recipe (mini buns, category: Side); servings inferred by summing total dough mass (~3460g) and dividing by an estimated ~38g per mini bun, giving ~90 pieces — flagged as an inference, not a stated count.
10 tbsp unsalted butter at ~14.2g/tbsp; kneaded into dough, remains in the eaten buns.
2 whole eggs (~50g each) listed, but instructions only use the egg white brushed on top as a glaze before baking — most of the egg mass is not actually consumed as part of the dough.
